englishDefinition: A small amount or to a small degree.
A film that is not exciting or interesting to watch.
Two or a small number of something.
A group or set of twelve.
A very small amount of liquid, often shaped like a tiny ball.

A large amount of something.
A whole baked piece of bread before it is sliced.
Two matching items used together, like shoes or socks.
One human being; an individual.
A part separated or broken off from a larger whole.
A mass of objects stacked or heaped together.
A piece of writing in short lines, often with rhythm and feeling.

Having the power, skill, or means to do something.
In or to a country other than your own.
Not present in a place such as school or work.
To agree to receive or take something.
Something bad that happens by chance, often causing harm.
To successfully reach a goal after working hard for it.
Something done successfully, often with skill or effort.
From one side of something to the other.
To do something or to behave in a certain way.
Something done; the process of doing things.
Busy, lively, and doing many things.
Something that people do for fun, work, or exercise.
A person who performs in plays, movies, or TV shows.
In fact; in reality, often used to correct an idea.
To put one thing together with another to make more.
The number, street, and city where someone lives or works.
To respect or like someone or something very much.
To say that something is true, often unwillingly.
To take a child or pet into your family as your own.
A picture, video, or text that tries to make people buy something.
Words that tell someone what they should do.
To change or have an influence on something.
Feeling fear or worry about something.
Opposite to, or not in support of, an idea or person.
In front; in advance; in a position further along.
To get off a vehicle such as a bus or train.
Money given regularly to a child by parents to spend.
How much there is of something.
A tale that holds the listener's attention and is fun to hear.
Belonging to a time long, long ago in the past.
The yearly date when something special happened, like a wedding.
Happening once every year.
A set of rooms where someone lives, in a larger building.
To say sorry for doing something wrong.
The way someone or something looks from the outside.
A question asking if someone is joking or being serious.
Things people make to look at, like paintings, drawings, or sculptures.
A picture, painting, or other creative piece made by an artist.
To help someone do something.
The act of helping someone with a task.
A person whose job is to help someone else, often a manager.
In the beginning, before things changed.
Finally, after a long time of waiting.
Not less than a certain amount; the minimum.
At twelve o'clock in the middle of the night.
The group of people watching or listening to a show.
Ready or able to be used, bought, or chosen.
A person paid to take care of children when parents are out.
The state of staying steady without falling over.
A group of people who play music together.
An act of washing the body in a tub of water.
Very pleasing to look at or to hear.
To think that something is true or real.
To be owned by someone or be a part of a group.
A long seat for two or more people, often placed outside.
A paper showing how much money you must pay for something.
The science that studies living things, like plants and animals.
The day each year when someone celebrates the date they were born.

Having a sharp, unpleasant taste, like coffee without sugar.

To say that someone is responsible for a fault or wrong.
Empty; with nothing written, drawn, or printed on it.
A thick soft cover used on a bed to keep warm.
Not able to see.

The red liquid that flows through the body of people and animals.

All the parts that make up a person or animal.

One of the hard pieces inside a body that gives it shape.

Feeling tired and uninterested because nothing is fun to do.
Not interesting; making people feel sleepy or uninterested.
Brought into life from the mother.
To take something for a short time, planning to give it back.
A person in charge of workers at a job.
The two together; one and also the other.
To annoy or disturb someone.
A tall round container with a narrow top, usually for liquids.
The lowest part of something.
A round, deep dish used for food like soup or rice.
A sport where you roll a heavy ball to knock down pins.
The organ inside the head that controls thinking and feeling.
Showing no fear when facing danger or hard things.
